vocabulary.sentence_spanish: El titular captó mi atención inmediatamente.

vocabulary.sentence: A rumour spread quickly on social media.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Un rumor se difundió rápidamente en redes sociales.

vocabulary.sentence: The company has a good reputation for quality.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: La empresa tiene buena reputación por su calidad.

vocabulary.sentence: Trust is essential in any relationship.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: La confianza es esencial en cualquier relación.

vocabulary.sentence: The conflict began with a misunderstanding.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: El conflicto empezó con un malentendido.

vocabulary.sentence: Different groups must cooperate to solve the problem.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Diferentes grupos deben cooperar para resolver el problema.

vocabulary.sentence: Speaking practice can increase your confidence.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: La práctica oral puede aumentar tu confianza en ti mismo.

vocabulary.sentence: Positive feedback can improve self-esteem.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Los comentarios positivos pueden mejorar la autoestima.

vocabulary.sentence: She is ambitious and wants to lead the project.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Es ambiciosa y quiere liderar el proyecto.

vocabulary.sentence: A reliable friend keeps promises.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Un amigo fiable cumple sus promesas.

vocabulary.sentence: It would be sensible to save some money.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Sería sensato ahorrar algo de dinero.

vocabulary.sentence: He is sensitive to criticism.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Es sensible a las críticas.

vocabulary.sentence: You need to be patient when learning a language.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Necesitas ser paciente al aprender un idioma.

vocabulary.sentence: The customers became impatient after waiting too long.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Los clientes se impacientaron después de esperar demasiado.

vocabulary.sentence: They were generous with their time and advice.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Fueron generosos con su tiempo y sus consejos.

vocabulary.sentence: It was selfish to ignore everyone else's needs.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Fue egoísta ignorar las necesidades de los demás.

vocabulary.sentence: That was a thoughtful message to send.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Fue un mensaje muy considerado.

vocabulary.sentence: He is too stubborn to admit his mistake.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Es demasiado terco para admitir su error.

vocabulary.sentence: An open-minded person listens to different opinions.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Una persona de mente abierta escucha opiniones diferentes.

vocabulary.sentence: She can be moody when she is tired.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Puede estar cambiante de humor cuando está cansada.

vocabulary.sentence: His cheerful attitude helped the team.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Su actitud alegre ayudó al equipo.

vocabulary.sentence: She was upset about the bad news.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Estaba disgustada por las malas noticias.

vocabulary.sentence: I felt relieved when I found my passport.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Me sentí aliviado cuando encontré mi pasaporte.

vocabulary.sentence: He felt embarrassed after forgetting her name.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Se sintió avergonzado después de olvidar su nombre.

vocabulary.sentence: She was ashamed of her behaviour.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Se avergonzó de su comportamiento.

vocabulary.sentence: They were proud of their daughter's success.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Estaban orgullosos del éxito de su hija.

vocabulary.sentence: He felt jealous when his friend won the prize.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Sintió celos cuando su amigo ganó el premio.

vocabulary.sentence: I get on well with my colleagues.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Me llevo bien con mis compañeros de trabajo.

vocabulary.sentence: She fell out with her best friend.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Se peleó con su mejor amiga.

vocabulary.sentence: They argued, but they made up later.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Discutieron, pero se reconciliaron después.

vocabulary.sentence: I do not want to let my team down.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: No quiero decepcionar a mi equipo.

vocabulary.sentence: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Aunque estaba lloviendo, salimos a caminar.

vocabulary.sentence: Even though the exam was difficult, she passed.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Aunque el examen era difícil, aprobó.

vocabulary.sentence: Some people prefer cities, whereas others prefer villages.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Algunas personas prefieren las ciudades, mientras que otras prefieren los pueblos.

vocabulary.sentence: The idea is interesting; however, it is expensive.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: La idea es interesante; sin embargo, es cara.

vocabulary.sentence: The roads were icy; therefore, school was closed.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Las carreteras estaban heladas; por lo tanto, la escuela cerró.

vocabulary.sentence: The hotel is cheap; moreover, it is near the centre.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: El hotel es barato; además, está cerca del centro.

vocabulary.sentence: In addition, the course includes speaking practice.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Además, el curso incluye práctica oral.

vocabulary.sentence: Prices rose and, as a result, sales fell.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Los precios subieron y, como resultado, las ventas bajaron.

vocabulary.sentence: The event was cancelled due to bad weather.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: El evento fue cancelado debido al mal tiempo.

vocabulary.sentence: In spite of the noise, I managed to sleep.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: A pesar del ruido, logré dormir.

vocabulary.sentence: Despite the delay, we arrived on time.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: A pesar del retraso, llegamos a tiempo.

vocabulary.sentence: You can borrow my car as long as you drive carefully.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Puedes tomar mi coche siempre que conduzcas con cuidado.

vocabulary.sentence: We will miss the train unless we hurry.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Perderemos el tren a menos que nos demos prisa.

vocabulary.sentence: Take an umbrella in case it rains.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Lleva un paraguas por si acaso llueve.

vocabulary.sentence: For instance, many teenagers use video apps daily.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Por ejemplo, muchos adolescentes usan aplicaciones de video a diario.

vocabulary.sentence: In contrast, the second study found different results.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: En cambio, el segundo estudio encontró resultados diferentes.

vocabulary.sentence: Compared with last year, sales have improved.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Comparado con el año pasado, las ventas han mejorado.

vocabulary.sentence: In terms of price, this option is better.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: En cuanto al precio, esta opción es mejor.

vocabulary.sentence: We must take into account the needs of older people.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Debemos tener en cuenta las necesidades de las personas mayores.

vocabulary.sentence: Bear in mind that the journey may take longer.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Ten presente que el viaje puede durar más.

vocabulary.sentence: Prices are likely to rise next year.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Es probable que los precios suban el año que viene.

vocabulary.sentence: You are supposed to arrive ten minutes early.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Se supone que debes llegar diez minutos antes.

vocabulary.sentence: She made an effort to speak more clearly.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Hizo un esfuerzo por hablar con más claridad.

vocabulary.sentence: Pay attention to the examples in the text.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Presta atención a los ejemplos del texto.

vocabulary.sentence: We promised to keep in touch after the course.
vocabulary.sentence_spanish: Prometimos mantener el contacto después del curso.
